title: दुर्गा गायत्री मन्त्र
devanagariText:
॥ श्री दुर्गा गायत्री मन्त्रः ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
सिंहस्थां शशिशेखरां त्रिनयनां पद्मासनस्थां शिवाम्
शूलं चक्रगदासिखेटधनुषः शङ्खं गदां बिभ्रतीम्।
नागहारभरांगभूषिततनुं कोट्यर्कप्राप्तभाम्
दुर्गादेवीमनन्तां सुरनरवन्दितां वन्दे जगद्धात्रिम्॥

ओं जगदम्बिकायै विद्महे दुर्गायै देव्यै धीमहि।
शक्तिरूपां स्मरामि नित्यम्॥

॥ दुर्गा गायत्री ॥
ॐ कात्यायन्यै विद्महे
कन्यकुमार्यै धीमहि।
तन्नो दुर्गिः प्रचोदयात्॥

॥ अपर दुर्गा गायत्री ॥
ॐ महादेव्यै च विद्महे
दुर्गायै च धीमहि।
तन्नो देवी प्रचोदयात्॥

॥ गिरिजा गायत्री ॥
ॐ गिरिजायै च विद्महे
शिवप्रियायै धीमहि।
तन्नो दुर्गा प्रचोदयात्॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
दुर्गागायत्रीमेतां यः पठेद् भक्तिमान्नरः।
सर्वापद्भ्यो विमुच्येत देवीलोकं स गच्छति॥

॥ इति श्री दुर्गा गायत्री मन्त्रः सम्पूर्णः ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Durga Gayatri Mantrah ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Simhasthaam Shashishekharaam Trinayanam Padmasanasthaam Shivam
Shoolam Chakra-gada-asi-kheta-dhanusah Shankham Gadam Bibhratim.
Naga-haara-bharanga-bhooshita-tanum Koti-arka-prapta-bhaam
Durga-devim-anantam Sura-nara-vanditaam Vande Jagaddhatrim.

Om Jagadambikayai Vidmahe Durgayai Devyai Dheemahi.
Shakti-roopam Smarami Nityam.

|| Durga Gayatri ||
Om Katyayanyai Vidmahe
Kanyakumaryai Dheemahi.
Tanno Durgih Prachodayat.

|| Apara Durga Gayatri ||
Om Mahadevyai Cha Vidmahe
Durgayai Cha Dheemahi.
Tanno Devi Prachodayat.

|| Girija Gayatri ||
Om Girijayai Cha Vidmahe
Shivapriyayai Dheemahi.
Tanno Durga Prachodayat.

|| Phalashruti ||
Durga-gayatrim-etam Yah Pathed Bhaktiman-narah.
Sarvapadbhyo Vimuchyeta Devi-lokam Sa Gachchhati.

|| Iti Shri Durga Gayatri Mantrah Sampurnah ||
hindiMeaning:
ध्यान: सिंहवाहिनी, शशिशेखरा, त्रिनयना, पद्मासनस्था, शूल-चक्र-गदा-खड्ग-ढाल-धनुष-शंख-गदा धारिणी, नागहार से भूषित, कोटि सूर्यों के समान प्रभावाली, अनन्ता, सुर-नर-वन्दिता जगद्धात्री दुर्गा को वन्दन।

दुर्गा गायत्री: हम कात्यायनी को जानते हैं, कन्याकुमारी का ध्यान करते हैं। वे दुर्गा हमारी बुद्धि को प्रेरित करें।

अपर गायत्री: हम महादेवी को जानते हैं, दुर्गा का ध्यान करते हैं। वे देवी हमें प्रेरित करें।

गिरिजा गायत्री: हम गिरिजा को जानते हैं, शिवप्रिया का ध्यान करते हैं। वे दुर्गा हमें प्रेरित करें।

जो भक्तिमान पुरुष इस दुर्गा गायत्री का पाठ करता है, वह सर्वापदाओं से मुक्त होकर देवीलोक को जाता है।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीदुर्गागायत्रीमन्त्रस्य मार्कण्डेय ऋषिः। गायत्री छन्दः। श्री दुर्गा (कात्यायनी) देवता। ॐ दुं बीजम्। दुर्गा शक्तिः। सर्वापदा निवारण कीलकम्। श्रीदुर्गाप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Three Durga Gayatri variations — Katyayani, Mahadevi, and Girija
Invokes liberation from all calamities and dangers
Powerful protection mantra for Navratri and Tuesdays/Fridays
Traditionally recited for removal of enemies and negative forces
Invokes the blessing of courage, strength, and divine mother's grace
Leads the devotee to Devi Loka
description: Durga Gayatri Mantra (दुर्गा गायत्री मन्त्र) is a powerful Vedic meditation mantra dedicated to Goddess Durga. This sacred composition is sourced from Durga Saptashati / Markandeya Purana and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to bestow courage, strength, and divine mother's grace as well as lead the devotee to Devi Loka. Additionally, it is known to destroy enemies and negative forces. This prayer is traditionally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri and Durga Puja to invoke divine grace. At 5 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 4 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Durga Gayatri Mantra in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Durga Gayatri Mantra with dhyanam in Sanskrit and Hindi meaning. Three Gayatri variations invoking Goddess Durga for protection and courage.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Durga Gayatri Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Durga Gayatri Mantra (दुर्गा गायत्री मन्त्र) is a Vedic-style 24-syllable meditation mantra dedicated to Goddess Durga, modelled on the structure of the original Savitri Gayatri (Om Bhur Bhuvah Svah, Tat Savitur Varenyam, Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi, Dhiyo yo nah Prachodayat). The classical Durga Gayatri reads: Om Katyayanyai Vidmahe, Kanyakumaryai Dhimahi, Tanno Durgih Prachodayat — meaning 'Om, may we know Katyayani, may we meditate on the Virgin Maiden, may that Durga inspire us'. The text preserves three classical variants — Katyayani Gayatri, Mahadevi/Apara Durga Gayatri, and Girija Gayatri — together with a dhyana shloka and phalashruti, drawn from the Markandeya Purana / Durga Saptashati tradition. (Sources: Markandeya Purana; Durga Saptashati appendices.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Katyayanyai Vidmahe Kanyakumaryai Dhimahi?
seoFaqs.answer: Om Katyayanyai Vidmahe, Kanyakumaryai Dhimahi, Tanno Durgih Prachodayat is the most widely recited form of the Durga Gayatri. Katyayani is the sixth of the Navadurga and the daughter (or daughter-form) of sage Katyayana — she is the warrior-goddess who slew Mahishasura according to the Devi Mahatmya. Kanyakumari is the eternally virgin (kumari = unmarried, also celebrated at Kanyakumari at India's southern tip) — the unmanifest power before manifestation. Durgi is Durga in the vocative form. The mantra means: We know (vidmahe) Katyayani, we meditate (dhimahi) on the Virgin Maiden, may that Durga inspire (prachodayat) us — paralleling the Savitri Gayatri's invocation of solar inspiration. (Sources: Devi Mahatmya; Tantric mantra tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the three Durga Gayatri variations in this text?
seoFaqs.answer: The text preserves three classical Durga Gayatri variants: (1) Katyayani Gayatri — Om Katyayanyai Vidmahe, Kanyakumaryai Dhimahi, Tanno Durgih Prachodayat — invoking the warrior-virgin form; (2) Apara Durga / Mahadevi Gayatri — Om Mahadevyai cha Vidmahe, Durgayai cha Dhimahi, Tanno Devi Prachodayat — invoking the supreme Goddess form; (3) Girija Gayatri — Om Girijayai cha Vidmahe, Shivapriyayai Dhimahi, Tanno Durga Prachodayat — invoking Durga as the Mountain-born (Parvati) and beloved of Shiva. Together they capture three theological aspects: the warrior, the supreme cosmic shakti, and the consort of Shiva — three faces of the same Devi. (Sources: classical mantra-shastra; Durga Saptashati tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Durga Gayatri Mantra recited and how many times?
seoFaqs.answer: The Durga Gayatri Mantra is auspicious for daily japa during sandhya (twilight) — like all Gayatris, it is traditionally chanted at sunrise, midday and sunset, ideally during Brahma muhurta. It is especially powerful on Tuesdays (Mangalvar — sacred to fierce goddesses) and Fridays (Shukravar — sacred to Devi), every Ashtami and Navami tithi (sacred to Durga), and during the four Navratris (Vasanta, Sharad, Magha-Gupta, Ashadha-Gupta) where japa of 108, 1008 or 10,008 repetitions is performed. The strict purashcharana for Durga Gayatri is 24 lakh (2.4 million) repetitions over a sustained sadhana — counted with rudraksha or sphatika mala, facing east or north. (Sources: classical mantra-purashcharana paddhati; Navratri vidhi manuals.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Durga Gayatri Mantra bestow according to its phalashruti?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti of the Durga Gayatri declares: durga-gayatrim-etam yah pathed bhaktiman-narah, sarvapadbhyo vimuchyeta devi-lokam sa gachchhati — 'whoever recites this Durga Gayatri with devotion is liberated from all calamities (sarva-apadbhyah) and goes to Devi-loka after death'. Traditional commentaries extend the benefits to: protection from enemies and negative forces, courage and confidence in the face of adversity, victory in legal and worldly conflicts, removal of obstacles, fulfilment of righteous desires, divine maternal grace and protection of children, success in spiritual sadhana, and ultimately the supreme phala of Devi-loka residence. The mantra is considered especially potent for women devotees seeking the Mother's protection. (Sources: Durga Gayatri phalashruti; Markandeya Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Katyayani and why is she invoked in the Durga Gayatri?
seoFaqs.answer: Katyayani is the sixth of the Navadurga (nine forms of Durga celebrated during Sharad Navratri's nine nights) and one of the most powerful warrior-forms of the Goddess. According to the Vamana, Markandeya and Skanda Puranas, she was born from the combined tejas of all the gods in the ashrama of sage Katyayana (hence Katyayani — 'daughter of Katyayana') specifically to slay the buffalo-demon Mahishasura. She is iconographically four or eighteen-armed, riding a lion, bearing the weapons given by all the devas. The Bhagavata Purana describes Katyayani Vrata performed by the gopis of Vrindavan in Margashirsha to obtain Krishna as husband, making her the patron goddess of unmarried girls seeking a good husband. (Sources: Devi Mahatmya 2; Bhagavata Purana 10.22; Vamana Purana.)
